summary: Design or refine a hiring pipeline with role criteria, candidate stages, and human-review checkpoints.

inputs.name: role_scope
inputs.description: Role definition, level, and hiring objective.

inputs.name: team_context
inputs.description: Interview capacity, stakeholders, and process constraints.

inputs.name: policy_constraints
inputs.description: Internal policy, onboarding needs, or region-specific cautions.

outputs.name: pipeline_design
outputs.description: Candidate stages, owners, and stage goals.

outputs.name: evaluation_framework
outputs.description: Role criteria, scorecard, and debrief structure.

outputs.name: onboarding_handoff_notes
outputs.description: Offer-to-onboarding transition checklist with human approval points.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve fairness and human decision ownership.

gates.rule: Confirm the request is process design or coordination support, not legal advice or autonomous hiring approval.
Require role criteria and team context before drafting interview stages.
Mark outputs as decision-support artifacts and include a human manager or HR-owner checkpoint.
Return clear stage ownership and note where policy, compensation, or offer decisions need human approval.

steps.action: Set role outcomes, interview goals, and stage-level evaluation criteria.
Lay out candidate stages, owners, timing expectations, and debrief moments.
Add offer-to-start coordination steps, documentation needs, and onboarding readiness checkpoints.
Merge the pipeline design and onboarding handoff into one reviewer-ready process pack.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read job profiles, process notes, and policy references.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Write a hiring process pack when requested.

verification.method: Check the workflow names role criteria before interview stages.
Check each pipeline stage has an owner or responsible team.
Check policy, offer, and onboarding approvals are explicitly human-owned.

success_criteria: The pipeline can be executed without guessing stage purpose or ownership.
Evaluation guidance is structured and consistent enough to reduce ad hoc decision making.
The output clearly states that hiring, policy, and offer decisions remain with human managers or HR leads.

rollback.when: If the pipeline omits role criteria or approval points, revert to the role definition and rebuild stage and onboarding steps.
